items.what_it_depicts: First page of Road & Track's February 1976 Countach LP400 road test — the 'Fastest Car We've Ever Tested' cover feature.
items.why_add: Actual scan of primary written source; anchors the room visually in its own citation.
items.true_original_source: Road & Track, February 1976 issue, Countach road test
items.chicago_citation: Road & Track, "Lamborghini Countach Road Test," Road & Track, February 1976, page scan via Curbside Classic, https://www.curbsideclassic.com/vintage-reviews/road-track-vintage-road-test-1976-lamborghini-countach-fastest-car-weve-ever-tested/.

items.what_it_depicts: Second page of the R&T February 1976 Countach test with instrumented performance data.
items.why_add: Shows the test data the exhibit's thesis rests on (0-60, top speed numbers).
items.true_original_source: Road & Track, February 1976 issue, Countach road test p. 2
items.chicago_citation: Road & Track, "Lamborghini Countach Road Test," Road & Track, February 1976, page 2, scan via Curbside Classic.

items.what_it_depicts: Pirelli factory advertising poster of the Countach in top-down view, April 1986.
items.why_add: Period factory advertising - a primary artifact of how Lamborghini + Pirelli marketed the car.
items.chicago_citation: Pirelli, Countach Top View Poster, April 1986, Wikimedia Commons (Public Domain).

items.what_it_depicts: Walter Wolf with his first Countach LP400 Speciale (chassis 1120148), c. 1976 — the red LP400 he commissioned with flared arches and rear wing.
items.why_add: Walter Wolf pioneered the wide-body look that became LP400S; period archival image.
items.photographer_studio: Lamborghini factory / Walter Wolf personal archive
items.chicago_citation: Lamborghini / Walter Wolf archive, "Walter Wolf with Countach LP400 Speciale," c. 1976, via LamboCars.com.

items.what_it_depicts: Walter Wolf-specification Countach, wide-body with flared arches and rear wing.
items.why_add: Physical example of the Wolf-spec car that drove the Countach's evolution; documents the archetype.
items.chicago_citation: contri, Lamborghini WOLF Countach, 2012, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Wolf Countach front-three-quarter showing the Wolf-livery scheme and gold rims.
items.why_add: Second angle of the Wolf-specification car — shows the badging and livery.
items.chicago_citation: contri, Lamborghini WOLF Countach, 2012, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: Jody Scheckter driving the Walter Wolf F1 team car at Brands Hatch, 1977 — Walter Wolf was simultaneously running his F1 team and commissioning custom Countachs.
items.why_add: Anchors the Walter Wolf story - he wasn't just a collector, he ran a Formula 1 team that finished 2nd in the 1977 Constructors' Championship.
items.chicago_citation: Gillfoto, Jody Scheckter in the Walter Wolf F1 at Brands Hatch, 1977, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0.

items.what_it_depicts: The 1989 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary chassis KLA12722 driven by Leonardo DiCaprio in The Wolf of Wall Street, sold by RM Sotheby's December 2023 for $1.655M.
items.why_add: Cultural-moment artifact: ties the Countach to contemporary cinema and sets a value benchmark.
items.true_original_source: RM Sotheby's New York, December 2023 sale press release
items.chicago_citation: RM Sotheby's, 1989 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary from The Wolf of Wall Street, December 2023 auction catalog.

items.what_it_depicts: Portrait of Marcello Gandini, Bertone's designer who penned the Countach LP500 prototype and LP400 production car, in 1976.
items.why_add: The designer whose lineage of work the exhibit documents; period portrait, public domain.
items.chicago_citation: Unknown photographer, Marcello Gandini in 1976, 1976, Wikimedia Commons (Public Domain).

items.what_it_depicts: Bertone founder Nuccio Bertone with the Lancia Stratos and Lamborghini Countach, mid-1970s, by Jesse Alexander.
items.why_add: Bertone and his two defining wedge-era designs in one frame; Jesse Alexander is a named automotive photographer.
items.chicago_citation: Jesse Alexander, Nuccio Bertone with the Stratos and Countach, c. 1974, Wikimedia Commons (Public Domain).

items.what_it_depicts: The Countach LP500 prototype reconstructed - the yellow Bertone car unveiled at the 1971 Geneva Motor Show (original destroyed in crash testing).
items.why_add: Fills the 1971 Geneva debut gap; the LP500 is the original design artifact Gandini drew.
items.chicago_citation: Dave Hamster, Lamborghini Countach LP500 Prototype, 2016, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0.

items.what_it_depicts: 1988 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary Edition in silver — the final evolution of the Countach, 1988-1990.
items.why_add: Documents the 25th Anniversary spec; fills the 1988 endpoint of the exhibit's timeline.
items.chicago_citation: MrWalkr, 1988 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary Silver, 2021, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 4.0.

items.why_add: Alternative angle of the 25th Anniversary spec; shows global collector context.
items.chicago_citation: Jones028, 1988 Lamborghini Countach 25th Anniversary Edition, Hong Kong, 2014, Wikimedia Commons, CC BY 2.0.
